page: Amazon Web Services m6g.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton processor. Utilizing the ARM64 architecture with Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz, this instance allocates dedicated physical cores with one thread per core. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, has a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieves 12135.21 in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and demonstrates solid memory bandwidth, reaching 472.85 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 120.79 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core and specialized execution capabilities represent a performance tradeoff. Single-core Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression is in the bottom 10%. Cryptography and compression workloads are also weak, as shown by a Passmark encryption score of 1083.15 and a gzip compression speed of 20.34 GB/sec.

The m6g.2xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 9.38 GB/sec, and database operations, processing 9.56 million Redis SET operations per second. It is also capable of software compilation tasks. Conversely, it is poorly suited for LLM inference, with text generation on a small model limited to 35.33 tokens/sec. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom ARM64 architecture delivers a balanced resource density for multi-threaded applications, though users must weigh this against lower single-threaded performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor using the ARM64 architecture. Running at 2.5 GHz with a dedicated core allocation and no hyperthreading, it is equipped with 32.0 GB of memory, a 2 Gbps baseline network, and no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12135.21 and strong cached memory read bandwidth of 472.85 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and specialized tasks like cryptography and LLM inference are weak, with Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. This instance is best suited for balanced workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations like Redis, offering a cost-efficient resource profile for multi-threaded applications that do not require high single-threaded throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.2xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 8 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.2xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64-based general-purpose instance delivering balanced multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers average multi-core performance, suitable for Redis and static web serving.
Exhibits weak single-core performance and low throughput for LLM inference.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.
